architecture_summary: PVT v2 improves the original Pyramid Vision Transformer (PVT v1) with three designs: a linear complexity attention layer, an overlapping patch embedding, and a convolutional feed-forward network. These changes reduce computational complexity to linearity and improve classification, detection, and segmentation performance.

novel_contributions: PVT v2, an improved framework over PVT v1 with 6 different size variants, from B0 to B5

key_claims.claim: PVT v2 improves the original PVT v1 with three orthogonal designs: linear complexity attention, overlapping patch embedding, and a convolutional feed-forward network.
PVT v2 reduces the computational complexity of PVT v1 to linearity and provides significant improvements on classification, detection, and segmentation.
PVT v2-B5 yields an 83.8% top-1 error on ImageNet, better than Swin-B and Twins-SVT-L while using fewer parameters and fewer GFLOPs.
GFL with PVT-B2 archives 50.2 AP on COCO 2017 val, 2.6 AP higher than Swin-T and 5.7 AP higher than ResNet50.
All classification models were trained for 300 epochs from scratch on 8 V100 GPUs with batch size 128.
PVT v2-B4 achieves 46.1 AP with RetinaNet and 47.5 AP^b with Mask R-CNN on COCO 2017 val.
PVT v2 consistently outperforms PVT v1 and other counterparts on ADE20K semantic segmentation.
